label: Reward modeling

node.label: Reward modeling
node.definition: Human or synthetic comparisons are converted into a learned scalar estimate of desirability.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Reward modeling is modeled as depending on Preference learning, Supervised learning and feeding downstream nodes such as Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF, Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops.

limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Preference learning, Supervised learning.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Reward modeling has a `uses` dependency on Preference learning because Preference learning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Reward modeling has a `uses` dependency on Supervised learning because Supervised learning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Reward modeling has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Margin, ranking & metric objectives because Margin, ranking & metric objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.

A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Preference learning, Supervised learning.
